content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Dianna M. Higdon 
city Clerk 
city Hall 
3954 Old Hamner 
P.O. Box 428 
Norco, CA 
91760 
Dear Ms. Higdon: 
August 4, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-405 
This is in response to your request for a confirmation of 
telephone advice concerning your responsibilities under the provi-
sions of the political Reform Act (the "Act").ll 
This letter 
confirms that your letter dated July 3, 1989 accurately summarizes 
the telephone advice I provided to you on June 22, 1989. 
As I stated in our telephone conversation, the Act requires 
that every report and statement filed pursuant to this title be a 
public record open for public inspection and reproduction. 
(Sec-
tion 81008(a).) 
section 81008(a) prohibits imposition of any 
conditions upon persons desiring to inspect or reproduce reports 
or statements filed under the Act, and limits the amount that may 
be charged for retrieval and reproduction of these records. 
It 
specifically prohibits requiring identification from persons seek-
ing to inspect or copy records. 
You stated in our telephone conversation that you have 
implemented a policy of asking for written requests for records in 
order to deal with the volume of requests to which your staff must 
respond. 
You also stated that you do not require identification 
or signatures with the requests. 
The policy as described is 
consistent with Section 81008 and with the Public Records Act. 
(Sections 6250, et seq.) 
Accordingly, we conclude that requiring 
written requests as described in your letter is reasonable and 
does not violate Act. 
However, you may wish to contact your city 
11 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


File No. A-89-405 
Page 2 
attorney in regard to other laws or regulations that might apply 
which are not within the jurisdiction of the Act or the Commis-
sion. 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
please feel free to contact this office at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:JWW:plh 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Dianna M. Higdon 
city Clerk 
P.O. Box 428 
Norco, CA 
91760 
Dear Ms. Higdon: 
July 12, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 405 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on July 6, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

sections.question: Due to the limited staff and the many requests for copies of records, we have a standard policy of asking that all requests be made in writing. This has proves a great time saver as it specifies the items desired. Recently we are in the process of a Recall and are having many requests for campaign statements and statements of economic interests, for 5 years of so. We have also asked that this be in writing, no need for signature or identification, and we comply with the FPPC regulations for time frame to acquire the information and the charges for copies. Upon a phone call Mr. John Wallace clarified that was an acceptable request, but since they had not had this situation previously I might want to write to get a written statement.
sections.conclusion: As I stated in our telephone conversation, the Act requires that every report and statement filed pursuant to this title be a public record open for public inspection and reproduction. (Section 81008(a).) section 81008(a) prohibits imposition of any conditions upon persons desiring to inspect or reproduce reports or statements filed under the Act, and limits the amount that may be charged for retrieval and reproduction of these records. It specifically prohibits requiring identification from persons seeking to inspect or copy records. You stated in our telephone conversation that you have implemented a policy of asking for written requests for records in order to deal with the volume of requests to which your staff must respond. You also stated that you do not require identification or signatures with the requests. The policy as described is consistent with Section 81008 and with the Public Records Act. (Sections 6250, et seq.) Accordingly, we conclude that requiring written requests as described in your letter is reasonable and does not violate Act.
sections.question_synthetic: May a city clerk's office implement a policy requiring written requests (without identification or signatures) for copies of campaign statements and economic interest statements under the Political Reform Act, particularly during a recall election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that requiring written requests for public records without demanding identification or signatures is consistent with Section 81008 of the Political Reform Act and the Public Records Act, and does not violate the Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter dated July 3, 1989, acknowledgment letter dated July 12, 1989, and formal FPPC response dated August 4, 1989. The August 4 letter is the primary advice letter confirming telephone advice. Multiple duplicate pages present in source document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Due to the limited staff and the many requests for copies of records, we have a standard policy of asking that all requests be made in writing. This has proves a great time saver as it specifies the items desired. Recently we are in the process of a Recall and are having many requests for campaign statements and statements of economic interests, for 5 years of so. We have also asked that this be in writing, no need for signature or identification, and we comply with the FPPC regulations for time frame to acquire the information and the charges for copies. Upon a phone call Mr. John Wallace clarified that was an acceptable request, but since they had not had this situation previously I might want to write to get a written statement.

CONCLUSION: As I stated in our telephone conversation, the Act requires that every report and statement filed pursuant to this title be a public record open for public inspection and reproduction. (Section 81008(a).) section 81008(a) prohibits imposition of any conditions upon persons desiring to inspect or reproduce reports or statements filed under the Act, and limits the amount that may be charged for retrieval and reproduction of these records. It specifically prohibits requiring identification from persons seeking to inspect or copy records. You stated in our telephone conversation that you have implemented a policy of asking for written requests for records in order to deal with the volume of requests to which your staff must respond. You also stated that you do not require identification or signatures with the requests. The policy as described is consistent with Section 81008 and with the Public Records Act. (Sections 6250, et seq.) Accordingly, we conclude that requiring written requests as described in your letter is reasonable and does not violate Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms prior telephone advice that the City of Norco's policy of requiring written (but not signed or identified) requests for copies of campaign statements and economic interest statements complies with the Political Reform Act's public records requirements.
